tasks.title: Normalize identifiers without breaking underscores
tasks.problem_statement:
Our service normalizes identifiers for internal lookup. Users report that identifiers with underscores are no longer found. Fix the bug with minimal changes.

Constraints:
- Keep behavior for hyphens and dots (strip them)
- Lowercase output
- Do not modify tests

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: underscores are semantic separators
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

We treat underscores as semantic separators in identifiers (e.g. Foo_Bar). Normalization must preserve underscores, otherwise lookups break.

Hyphens and dots are considered punctuation and should be stripped.

tasks.title: Implement org policy code from decision trace
tasks.problem_statement:
Our repo includes a compliance check that validates the org policy code via a one-way hash. The code itself is not derivable from the codebase.

Implement `get_policy_code()` so tests pass.

Important:
- The policy code is recorded in the Watercooler decision trace seeded for this task under the thread topic `custom-bench-org-knowledge`.
- If you have wc tools enabled, retrieve it explicitly (recommended: `wc-read-thread custom-bench-org-knowledge`).

Constraints:
- Minimal change
- Do not modify tests

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: current policy code
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

The current policy code is:

`WCCLOUD-POLICY-Z7Y4-8C2F`

This value is externally governed and should be returned by `custom_bench_app.policy.get_policy_code()`.

tasks.title: Implement policy and return citations
tasks.problem_statement:
Our system enforces an org policy (region + retention) that is governed by decisions recorded in Watercooler.

Implement `custom_bench_app.decision_trace.get_region_retention_policy()` and `get_region_retention_policy_citations()` so tests pass.

Important:
- The policy facts are split across multiple decision entries seeded under the thread topic `custom-bench-org-knowledge`.
- If you have wc tools enabled, retrieve the thread and use the entry IDs as citations (recommended: `wc-read-thread custom-bench-org-knowledge`).

Constraints:
- Minimal change
- Do not modify tests

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: region for retention policy
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

The retention policy region is:

`us-west-2`

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: retention days for region policy
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

The retention period (days) is:

`30`

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: required output format
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

The required output format for `get_region_retention_policy()` is:

`REGION=<region>;RETENTION_DAYS=<days>`

`get_region_retention_policy_citations()` must return the entry IDs that justify the facts used.

tasks.title: Discover policy across thread boundaries
tasks.problem_statement:
Implement `custom_bench_app.policy.get_policy_code()` so tests pass.

Important:
- The active policy code lives in a Watercooler thread topic that is NOT the default topic for this task.
- You should discover the right topic first (smart-query preferred), then read the relevant entry.
- A stale code exists in the default thread and must not be used.

Constraints:
- Minimal change
- Do not modify tests

threads_seed_entries.title: Deprecated policy code
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

Deprecated code (do not use): `WCCLOUD-POLICY-OLD-0000`.

tasks.title: Rehydrate full context after reset
tasks.problem_statement:
You will complete this task in two phases.

Goal: implement both `get_region_retention_policy()` and `get_region_retention_policy_citations()` so tests pass.

Phase 1:
- Gather the required facts and output format from Watercooler.
- Do NOT modify files.
- Write one wc-say note containing: region, retention days, required output format, and citation IDs.
- Then respond with SUBMIT.

Phase 2:
- Start from clean repo state.
- Rehydrate context from your Phase 1 handoff note and decisions.
- Implement the fix and run tests.

Constraints:
- Minimal change
- Do not modify tests

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: region for retention policy
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

The retention policy region is `us-west-2`.

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: retention days for region policy
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

The retention period is `30` days.

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: required output format
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

`get_region_retention_policy()` must return `REGION=<region>;RETENTION_DAYS=<days>` and citations must include the two fact entry IDs.

tasks.title: Use distilled policy facts with exact citations
tasks.problem_statement:
Implement `custom_bench_app.decision_trace.get_region_retention_policy()` and `get_region_retention_policy_citations()` so tests pass.

Important:
- A Watercooler summary note exists, but you must cite source decision IDs for factual claims.
- Do not rely on uncited summaries.

Constraints:
- Minimal change
- Do not modify tests

threads_seed_entries.title: Summary: region and retention
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

Summary: `REGION=us-west-2;RETENTION_DAYS=30`. Use source decision IDs for citations.

tasks.title: Resume after reset using Watercooler notes
tasks.problem_statement:
You will complete this task in two phases.

Goal: Fix the underscore regression in identifier normalization so tests pass.

Phase 1:
- Investigate and determine the exact minimal code change needed.
- Do NOT modify any files.
- Write your findings into Watercooler using `wc-say` (include: file path, exact change, and test command).
- Then respond with SUBMIT.

Phase 2:
- You are starting from a clean repo state (your code changes from Phase 1 are discarded).
- Retrieve your Phase 1 note using `wc-read-thread custom-bench-org-knowledge`.
- Apply the fix and run tests.

Constraints:
- Minimal change
- Do not modify tests

threads_seed_entries.title: Decision: underscores are semantic separators
threads_seed_entries.body:
Spec: planner-architecture

We treat underscores as semantic separators in identifiers (e.g. Foo_Bar). Normalization must preserve underscores, otherwise lookups break.

Hyphens and dots are considered punctuation and should be stripped.
